rules.name: Structuring — sub-threshold transfers
rules.description: Multiple transfers just below reporting or internal review thresholds within a rolling window.
rules.trigger: ≥3 outbound transfers in 7d where each amount is 85–99% of tier threshold and same beneficiary cluster
rules.thresholds: Tier: AED 50k retail / AED 200k SME · Window: 7d · Min count: 3
rules.action: Hold + analyst review · step-up authentication

rules.name: Smurfing — inbound fragmentation
rules.description: Many small inbound payments aggregating to material value from unrelated senders.
rules.trigger: ≥5 inbound transfers <AED 5k in 48h from ≥4 distinct senders · aggregate >AED 25k
rules.action: Alert L2 · Mohsen case prep

rules.name: Round-amount layering
rules.description: Perfectly round inbound amounts moved out rapidly — classic layering (Investigation Hub S-12 pattern).
rules.trigger: Inbound round amounts (50k/100k/200k AED or USD equiv) with ≥80% outbound within 24h
rules.action: Block further outbound · immediate L2 · SAR consideration

rules.name: Pass-through mule ratio
rules.description: Funds exit account within 48h without economic rationale vs declared activity.
rules.trigger: Pass-through ratio >60% (medium) or >80% within 24h (high) vs 90d baseline
rules.action: Enhanced monitoring · restrict new beneficiaries

rules.name: Velocity spike — transfers
rules.description: Transaction count or value exceeds CRA-expected band within rolling 7d window.
rules.trigger: Actual monthly band exceeded by ≥2 bands OR >3× declared expected activity
rules.action: Alert · CRAM feed transaction_alert

rules.name: Card spend velocity
rules.description: Card authorisation count or value spike vs customer profile and historical pattern.
rules.trigger: >10 card txs in 1h OR daily card spend >3× 90d average
rules.action: Step-up 3DS · soft block high-risk MCC

rules.name: New beneficiary burst
rules.description: Rapid addition of new transfer beneficiaries — mule recruitment indicator.
rules.trigger: 3–5 new beneficiaries in 7d (medium) · >5 in 7d or large first payment (high)
rules.action: Step-up auth · hold first large payment to new beneficiary

rules.name: High-risk corridor first use
rules.description: First payment to high-risk or non-declared corridor country.
rules.trigger: Corridor not in declared SoF/SoW countries · FATF grey/black list adjacency
rules.thresholds: Any amount · First occurrence · CRA High = any cross-border alert
rules.action: Hold · EDD trigger · geography rescore

rules.name: US BaaS corridor — FinCEN path
rules.description: US-licensed BaaS customer cross-border transfer requiring FinCEN-aligned monitoring.
rules.trigger: licenseRegion=US · cross-border outbound · amount >USD 3k
rules.action: Enhanced screening · US SAR workflow if escalated

rules.name: Sanctions name proximity — realtime
rules.description: Realtime payment counterparty matches sanctions/TFS list — mirror to Vital4, never write CRAM directly.
rules.trigger: Oscilar list screen ≥ potential match on sender, beneficiary, or merchant
rules.action: Block settlement · Vital4 mirror · hard stop OVR-001 path

rules.name: TF evasion — charitable front
rules.description: Payments to high-risk NGOs / charitable fronts in conflict-adjacent corridors.
rules.trigger: Beneficiary type=NGO · corridor high-risk · amount >AED 10k
rules.action: Hold · adverse media refresh · MLRO review

rules.name: Card CNP fraud — high-risk MCC
rules.description: Card-not-present spend at high-risk merchant category codes inconsistent with profile.
rules.trigger: CNP auth · MCC in {7995 gambling, 6012 crypto, 5967 telemarketing} · CRA Low/Medium
rules.action: Decline or step-up 3DS · device check

rules.name: Card testing / micro-charges
rules.description: Sequence of small card authorisations testing stolen PAN validity.
rules.trigger: ≥5 card auths <AED 10 in 30min · ≥3 distinct merchants
rules.action: Block card · fraud case · customer contact

rules.name: Chargeback / refund abuse
rules.description: Elevated chargeback or refund rate vs merchant or customer baseline.
rules.trigger: Chargeback rate >1% (medium) or >3% (high) rolling 30d
rules.action: Merchant review · customer rescore

rules.name: Cross-border card spend
rules.description: Card used in country not aligned with customer residence or travel pattern.
rules.trigger: Merchant country ≠ residence · no travel notification · amount >AED 5k
rules.action: Step-up 3DS · push notification confirm

rules.name: Funnel account — fan-in
rules.description: Many unrelated senders funding one account (mule collector).
rules.trigger: ≥8 distinct senders inbound in 14d · aggregate >AED 100k
rules.action: Restrict outbound · L2 investigation

rules.name: Funnel account — fan-out
rules.description: One account distributing to many beneficiaries rapidly.
rules.trigger: ≥8 distinct beneficiaries outbound in 7d · pass-through >70%
rules.action: Hold outbound · SAR typology flag

rules.name: Dormant account reactivation
rules.description: Account inactive >90d resumes material or high-risk activity.
rules.trigger: Dormant >90d · first txn >AED 20k OR high-risk corridor/MCC
rules.action: Event review · rescore · step-up

rules.name: Income inconsistency — transfer
rules.description: Observed turnover inconsistent with declared salary / SoF (Mohsen investigation pattern).
rules.trigger: 90d turnover >22× declared monthly salary OR >3× expected band
rules.action: L2 case · OVR-020 floor review

rules.name: Third-party funding
rules.description: Repeated funding from unrelated third parties inconsistent with profile.
rules.trigger: ≥3 third-party inbound in 30d · not salary/treasury tagged
rules.action: EDD · SoF documentation request

rules.name: Crypto / VA off-ramp
rules.description: Fiat in/out linked to VASP or crypto MCC — unlicensed VA exposure.
rules.trigger: Counterparty VASP flag OR MCC 6051/6012 · amount >AED 5k
rules.action: Hold · licensing check · prohibited if unlicensed

rules.name: PEP enhanced monitoring — transfer
rules.description: PEP customer transfer monitoring — Foreign PEP: all outbound; Domestic/IO PEP: cross-border or >AED 50k domestic (CBUAE Art. 15 · risk-based).
rules.trigger: CRA PEP ≠ None · (Foreign: any transfer OR cross-border) · (Domestic/IO: cross-border OR >AED 50k domestic)
rules.thresholds: Foreign PEP: any amount · Domestic/IO: cross-border any amount or AED 50k domestic
rules.action: Analyst notification · EDD workflow

rules.name: High CRA customer — any alert
rules.description: High or Prohibited CRA — lower alert thresholds, no auto-close.
rules.trigger: CRA rating High or Prohibited · any medium+ Oscilar score
rules.action: Mandatory analyst review · no whitelist without MLRO

rules.name: ATM withdrawal structuring
rules.description: Multiple ATM withdrawals just below daily limit.
rules.trigger: ≥4 ATM withdrawals in 24h · each 85–99% of daily ATM limit
rules.action: Alert · cap ATM limit

rules.name: Merchant collusion — round settlements
rules.description: Merchant settlement pattern inconsistent with declared business (round amounts, off-hours).
rules.trigger: SME merchant · round settlement amounts · off-hours batch
rules.action: Merchant hold · site visit trigger

rules.name: Instant transfer — salary mismatch
rules.description: Instant outbound transfer > declared monthly salary without HNW/SME justification.
rules.trigger: Single transfer >1.5× declared monthly salary · segment Retail
rules.action: Soft hold · push SoF confirmation

rules.name: Device sharing — multi-customer
rules.description: Same device fingerprint seen across multiple customer accounts in 7d.
rules.trigger: Device ID linked to ≥3 customerIds in 7d · any payment activity
rules.action: Block device · link cases · mule typology

rules.name: Watchlist internal — prior exit
rules.description: Customer or counterparty on internal watchlist (prior exit, fraud, rejected onboarding).
rules.trigger: Internal watchlist match · any payment attempt
rules.action: Block · OVR internal watchlist path

rules.name: P2P rapid cycling
rules.description: P2P transfers between small network of accounts cycling funds.
rules.trigger: Circular transfer pattern detected in ≤5 account network · 72h window
rules.action: Network case · group alerts

rules.name: Remittance corridor velocity — South Asia
rules.description: Elevated remittance velocity to PK/BD/IN/EG corridors vs declared remittance profile.
rules.trigger: Corridor in {PK,BD,IN,EG} · >3× 90d average monthly volume
rules.action: Alert · corridor documentation

rules.name: Hawala / hundi IVTS leg — UAE→PK
rules.description: Account acts as USD leg of informal value-transfer: assorted senders in, fan-out to unrelated PK beneficiaries. IVTS prohibition — authorized channel only.
rules.trigger: Corridor=PK · ≥4 distinct senders inbound in 14d · ≥3 unrelated PK beneficiaries outbound in 7d · pass-through >65%
rules.action: Hold outbound · IVTS investigation · authorized-channel-only payout

rules.name: TBML fictitious invoice — PK corridor
rules.description: Self-generated or reused invoices; intangible-service analogue of trade-based ML on UAE→PK remittance / SoF.
rules.trigger: Corridor=PK · purpose code service/export · invoice hash reused OR intangible service >AED 50k without contract ref
rules.action: Hold · SoF/invoice documentation · purpose-code integrity check

rules.name: Sanctions geolocation / VPN evasion — PK
rules.description: PK-linked customer with VPN/geolocation mismatch; name-component omission or spelling change after prior hit; intermediary routing.
rules.trigger: Residence/nationality PK · IP geolocation mismatch >500km · OR name token dropped post-screen · OR intermediary jurisdiction hop
rules.thresholds: Geo delta: 500km · Any prior near-miss in 90d
rules.action: Block settlement · geolocation control · fuzzy re-match · free-text remittance screen

rules.name: TF aggregation — KPK / Balochistan
rules.description: Small-value aggregation-then-forwarding to PK high-risk provinces; charity/NPO purpose abuse.
rules.trigger: Beneficiary province in {KPK, Balochistan} · ≥5 inbound <AED 5k in 30d · aggregate >AED 25k · OR NGO/charity beneficiary
rules.thresholds: Count: 5 · Aggregate: AED 25k · Provinces: KPK, Balochistan
rules.action: Hold · enhanced TF screening · NPO/charity = automatic EDD

rules.name: NTN / filer purpose-code — PK remittance
rules.description: Large remittances to tax-unregistered PK individuals; remittance vs service-export misclassification (FBR pattern).
rules.trigger: Corridor=PK · beneficiary non-filer/NTN missing · amount >AED 25k · purpose code remittance vs declared service export
rules.action: Hold · NTN/filer validation · purpose-code integrity · corridor trigger event

rules.name: RTRA fraud proceeds — mule/victim overlap
rules.description: Romance / investment / job / BEC / APP scam proceeds — customer double-exposed as mule and victim; disputed funds.
rules.trigger: Inbound tagged fraud/dispute OR RTRA score ≥85 · rapid outbound within 24h · corridor PK
rules.action: Pre-facto block credit · fraud/ATO fast path · Reg E path if US leg

rules.name: Elderly / coerced transfer — PK outbound
rules.description: Age-gap patterns, surname mismatch, relationship inconsistent with transaction; sender acting on third-party instruction.
rules.trigger: Sender age ≥65 · beneficiary surname mismatch · relationship field inconsistent · OR third-party instruction flag
rules.thresholds: Age: 65+ · Surname match required · Relationship payload
rules.action: Pre-facto hold · frontline judgement · age/surname/relationship payload to TSM

rules.name: Sanctioned wallet cluster — mixer exposure
rules.description: Direct or indirect exposure to terrorism/sanctions-tagged wallet clusters; mixer-routed funds on USDC treasury leg.
rules.trigger: Blockchain analytics ≥10% indirect exposure to tagged cluster within ≤5 hops · OR mixer route detected
rules.action: Auto-block · blockchain analytics · GA-5/SCP §3.3

rules.name: Synthetic identity — CNIC / document fraud
rules.description: Document-fraud indicators, duplicate or shared CNIC/NICOP elements across customers at onboarding.
rules.trigger: Shufti discrepancy OR duplicate CNIC hash OR shared identity element across ≥2 customerIds
rules.action: Block onboarding · re-verification · IDV escalation

rules.name: Wallet-tier structuring — JazzCash / Easypaisa
rules.description: Transactions sized to stay under JazzCash/Easypaisa mobile-wallet tier limits on PK payout rail.
rules.trigger: Corridor=PK · beneficiary wallet rail {JazzCash,Easypaisa} · ≥3 transfers in 7d each 85–99% of wallet tier limit
rules.action: Alert · structuring SAR · wallet-tier limits enforced

rules.name: Self-transfer own-account — CoP name-mismatch
rules.description: Same-name self-transfer to the customer's own account elsewhere fails Confirmation of Payee (name mismatch) — 'own account' used to break the audit trail before dispersal.
rules.trigger: Self-transfer (C2C-12 / M2M-01) where receiving-side beneficiary name != account holder (CoP fail)
rules.thresholds: Same-name CoP required · hard control (not evidence)
rules.action: Block / review transfer · investigate layering pre-stage

rules.name: Self-transfer velocity — layering pre-stage
rules.description: Inbound third-party credit(s) immediately followed by a same-name self-transfer out — self-transfer velocity feeding a layering pre-stage.
rules.trigger: >=1 unrelated third-party inbound credit within 48h before a same-name self-transfer out · self-transfer velocity above declared profile
rules.action: Step-up · RFI on purpose

rules.name: Charity-to-individual fan-in — unlicensed fundraising / TF
rules.description: Crowd-collection into one individual (C2C-13): small-value aggregation then onward to conflict-adjacent / opaque beneficiaries — unlicensed fundraising / TF risk.
rules.trigger: Multiple unrelated inbound 'charitable' credits aggregating into one individual · onward to high-risk / conflict-adjacent corridor
rules.thresholds: Low per-txn cap · aggregation review · corridor restrictions
rules.action: EDD + STR · rapid reporting · decline crowd-collection use

rules.name: Parallel-market FX structuring — EG / PK
rules.description: Sub-threshold clustering timed to FX-rate gaps; structuring around FX limits; USD-cash conversion pressure (EG/PK parallel-market FX).
rules.trigger: Corridor in {EG,PK} · sub-threshold clusters timed to FX-rate movements · USD-cash conversion pattern
rules.action: SAR · step-up · CTR on aggregate

rules.name: Purpose-vs-behaviour divergence
rules.description: Observed pattern diverges from declared expected-activity purpose; purpose-code switching or 'Other'-rate spike — classification avoidance.
rules.trigger: Observed counterparties / amounts / frequency != declared purpose · purpose-code switching · 'Other'-rate spike
rules.thresholds: Divergence vs declared baseline · KRI review
rules.action: KRI review · free-text keyword scan · step-up / RFI

rules.name: Distributed structuring across multiple MSBs / wallets
rules.description: Same customer uses multiple MSBs / wallets for one route to distribute value below single-institution detection thresholds.
rules.trigger: Same customer / route observed across multiple MSBs or wallets · aggregate exceeds single-institution thresholds
rules.action: Aggregate monitoring · SAR assessment

rules.name: MCC-vs-purpose mismatch — transaction laundering (C2B)
rules.description: C2B goods/services payment to a payee whose merchant category or trade does not match the declared purpose — payee has no matching trade footprint.
rules.trigger: C2B purpose (goods/services) != beneficiary MCC/declared trade; goods payment to a payee with no goods trade
rules.thresholds: MCC-vs-purpose cross-check · above-tier invoice on request
rules.action: Step-up · RFI for invoice · re-classify

rules.name: Fake-invoice / fake-service payment (C2B)
rules.description: Round-amount recurring 'services' payments to a consulting/shell-type business with no deliverable — value-transfer laundering dressed as a service (TYP-PK-003).
rules.trigger: C2B-02 services · round-amount + recurrence conjunction · no service footprint · circular payments
rules.action: EDD · RFI engagement + deliverable · SAR if no substance

rules.name: Crypto-exchange funding — unlicensed / off-whitelist VASP (C2B)
rules.description: C2B-12 crypto-exchange funding where the VASP is not on the licensed-VASP whitelist, or funding shifts off-whitelist; rapid in-convert-out conduit shape.
rules.trigger: C2B-12 · beneficiary VASP not on licensed-VASP whitelist · same-name fail · velocity above tier cap
rules.thresholds: Licensed-VASP whitelist gate · same-name · velocity caps by tier
rules.action: Decline the payment (whitelist gate); block on sanctioned-wallet exposure

rules.name: Investment / brokerage funding to an unregulated beneficiary (C2B)
rules.description: C2B-11 investment/brokerage funding to an unregulated beneficiary; many customers fund the same new business (fan-in) — investment-scam / unlicensed deposit-taking shape.
rules.trigger: C2B-11 · beneficiary not licensed/whitelisted platform · many-customers→same-new-business fan-in · off-profile funding
rules.thresholds: Platform licence/whitelist check · same-name preferred · fan-in detection
rules.action: Decline (unregulated beneficiary) · scheme review · SAR on scheme indicators

rules.name: Payment to a prohibited-end-type business (C2B)
rules.description: C2B payment to a prohibited business end-type — gaming/casino/POGO, real-estate/construction, DPMS/gold, unlicensed MSB/hawala, unlicensed lender.
rules.trigger: Beneficiary business is a prohibited end-type (§6.4) · personal account funding a prohibited business
rules.action: Decline the payment; EDD + SAR on the individual's exposure

rules.name: Charity donation — licensed-charity whitelist gate / NPO-diversion (C2B)
rules.description: C2B-10 charity donation where the charity is not on the licensed-charity whitelist, or an NPO-diversion / conflict-adjacent-beneficiary pattern.
rules.trigger: C2B-10 · charity not on licensed-charity whitelist · high-risk-geography NPO · charity onward flows to conflict-adjacent zones
rules.thresholds: Licensed-charity whitelist (hard control) · NPO-diversion overlay · per-period cap
rules.action: Decline (unlicensed/conflict-adjacent) · STR + rapid reporting

rules.name: Ghost-employee / fake-payroll fan-out (B2C)
rules.description: B2C payroll where WPS/payroll payee names diverge from the verified roster, headcount is implausible for turnover, or salaries are funnelled to a common account — ghost employees or a salary-label remittance funnel.
rules.trigger: Payroll names != verified roster/WPS SIF; payroll >> declared headcount; multiple salaries routed to one account; salary-labelled remittance velocity
rules.thresholds: Roster-to-payroll reconciliation · headcount-vs-turnover plausibility · funnel-to-common-account detection
rules.action: Step-up · roster reconciliation RFI · SAR on confirmed ghost-payroll / funnel

rules.name: Commission / agent payout — kickback & corruption overlay (B2C)
rules.description: B2C-09 commission or agent payout to individuals with no underlying-sales linkage, an implausible commission-to-revenue ratio, or PEP-linked payees — a corruption/kickback conduit.
rules.trigger: Commission with no documented sales linkage; commission-to-revenue ratio implausible; commission/agent payee is PEP-linked or state-linked
rules.thresholds: Commission-to-revenue ratio · sales-linkage evidence · PEP screening of beneficiaries (corruption overlay)
rules.action: EDD + corroborated SoW + senior sign-off (PEP) · SAR + decline on confirmed kickback

rules.name: Dividend / distribution to a non-shareholder — hidden-ownership signal (B2C)
rules.description: B2C-10 dividend paid to an individual not on the shareholder registry, or dividends grossly exceeding plausible profits — a hidden-ownership / disguised-distribution signal; also fires on unresolvable-UBO / nominee-front structures.
rules.trigger: Dividend payee not registry-matched; dividends >> plausible profits; control-vs-declared mismatch; nominee/front markers on the entity
rules.thresholds: Shareholder-registry match (hard control) · profit plausibility · UBO refresh on mismatch
rules.action: Escalate · UBO refresh · SAR on hidden ownership · refuse if UBO becomes unresolvable

rules.name: Refund fan-out — refund laundering (B2C)
rules.description: B2C-11 refunds to individuals with no matching original inbound sale, refunds fanned out to unrelated individuals, or refund volume disproportionate to sales — a refund-laundering channel.
rules.trigger: Refund with no paired inbound sale; refund fan-out to unrelated individuals; refunds >> sales volume
rules.thresholds: Systemic refund-to-sale pairing · refund-ratio baseline · beneficiary coherence
rules.action: Enforce refund-pairing · step-up · SAR on unpaired refund fan-out

rules.name: Loan disbursement by an unlicensed lender — unregistered MSB (B2C)
rules.description: B2C-13 loan disbursements to individuals by an entity with no lending licence — serial disbursements, loan-back round-trips, pass-through shape; treated as unlicensed lending / unregistered MSB.
rules.trigger: Loan disbursement selected by a non-licensed entity (licence-verification hard gate fails); serial disbursements to many individuals; loan-back round-trips; pass-through
rules.thresholds: Lending-licence verification (hard gate) · serial-disbursement + round-trip detection
rules.action: Decline + escalate + SAR (unlicensed lending / unregistered money transmission, §6.4)

rules.name: Prize / promotion payout — lottery-scam & TF cover (B2C)
rules.description: B2C-15 prize/promotion payouts used as disguised transfers, without a promotion permit, fanned out beyond genuine winners, or to conflict-adjacent individuals — lottery-scam or TF cover.
rules.trigger: 'Prizes' as disguised transfers; no promotion permit; prize-payout fan-out beyond capped genuine winners; payees in conflict-adjacent regions
rules.thresholds: Promotion-permit gate · beneficiary + frequency caps · payee/region TF screening
rules.action: Decline/SAR on scam-pattern or no permit · STR + rapid reporting on TF indicators

rules.name: Trade-based ML — over/under-invoicing / phantom shipment (B2B)
rules.description: B2B goods/import/export payment where invoice value, goods description and shipping/customs docs do not reconcile — over/under-invoicing, phantom shipments, unit-price outliers, or payment to a third country unrelated to the trade.
rules.trigger: Invoice != goods/shipping; unit-price outliers; payment to a third country unrelated to trade; 3rd-party (non-invoice) payer; dual-use goods
rules.thresholds: Trade-doc reconciliation (invoice + BoL + customs) · unit-price outlier tests · customs-duty vs import-volume consistency · 3rd-party-payment detection
rules.action: Trade-doc RFI · reconcile · decline the flow + SAR on TBML (scenario 5.15)

rules.name: Related-party / circular trade round-trip (B2B)
rules.description: B2B trade/intercompany flow between entities under common control — inflated related-party invoicing, circular supplier rings, or intra-group round-trips that manufacture turnover (gross ≫ net).
rules.trigger: Payer & counterparty under common control; circular supplier rings; undeclared 'group' transfers; gross ≫ net; round-amount invoice grids
rules.thresholds: Related-party / common-control test · group-relationship registry (intercompany) · netting analysis · circular-flow detection (TM 5.9)
rules.action: Related-party review · registry check · decline the flow + SAR on round-trip / no lawful purpose

rules.name: Shell-payment consulting/management fees to a secrecy jurisdiction (B2B)
rules.description: B2B-04 professional/consulting/management fees to a counterparty in a low-transparency/secrecy jurisdiction with no deliverable footprint — a shell-payment value-transfer label.
rules.trigger: Vague 'consulting/management fees'; no deliverable; counterparty in a secrecy jurisdiction; consulting-fee share of outflows dominant
rules.thresholds: Engagement letter + deliverable evidence · counterparty existence/KYB · secrecy-jurisdiction flag · consulting-fee-share analytics
rules.action: EDD · deliverable RFI · decline the flow + SAR on no-substance

rules.name: Intercompany / dividend / capital round-trip — hidden ownership (B2B)
rules.typology: Hidden ownership / UBO-chain mismatch / capital round-tripping
rules.description: B2B dividend, intercompany or capital-injection flow where the corporate counterparty falls outside the declared ownership chain, the UBO-chain match fails, or capital round-trips into a shell — a hidden-ownership signal; also fires on unresolvable-UBO / nominee-front payers.
rules.trigger: Dividend/capital counterparty outside declared UBO chain; UBO-chain match fails; 'investments' into shells; capital round-tripping; nominee/front markers
rules.thresholds: UBO-chain match (hard control, dividends) · investee UBO refresh (capital) · board resolution · round-trip detection · ownership-inconsistency (TM 5.23)
rules.action: Escalate · UBO refresh · SAR on hidden ownership / round-trip · refuse if payer UBO unresolvable

rules.name: Commission / agency-fee kickback to a shell intermediary (B2B)
rules.description: B2B-12 commission/agency fee (or B2B-14 construction progress payment) to an intermediary with no underlying-transaction linkage, or to a PEP-linked / state-linked principal — a bribery/kickback or procurement-corruption conduit.
rules.trigger: Agency fees to shell intermediaries; no underlying transaction; PEP-linked / state-linked principals; procurement-corruption patterns; progress payments without project evidence
rules.thresholds: Agreement + underlying-transaction linkage · intermediary KYB / intermediary-web analysis · PEP screening of principals · commission-to-revenue ratio · procurement red-flag overlay
rules.action: EDD + senior sign-off (PEP) · SAR + escalate on kickback / procurement corruption

rules.name: Royalty / IP-fee mispricing — profit-shifting (B2B)
rules.description: B2B-13 royalty/IP fee to a related or low-transparency counterparty with pricing outliers — a transfer-pricing / profit-shifting channel; also captures software-licence-as-IP-mispricing (B2B-05).
rules.trigger: IP fees to low-transparency jurisdictions; pricing outliers vs arm's-length; related-party licence; software-licence-as-IP mispricing
rules.thresholds: Licence agreement · pricing basis · transfer-pricing outlier tests · secrecy-jurisdiction flag · related-party check
rules.action: EDD · transfer-pricing review · escalate/decline + SAR on mispricing / profit-shifting

rules.name: Internal-layering chain — on-us A→B→C→D then out (Mal2Mal)
rules.description: On-us funds injected to account A and swept A→B→C→D across Mal accounts, then out via an external cash-out leg — each hop looks small and clean, but the aggregate chain is layering. The signature on-us typology: aggregate the chain, do not score each hop in isolation.
rules.trigger: Injection → multi-account on-us sweep → external cash-out; short dwell time per hop; on-us chain-depth + velocity + pass-through (in-then-straight-out); no economic rationale per hop
rules.thresholds: Aggregate chain reconstruction (not per-hop) · chain-depth · on-us velocity · pass-through (in-then-out) · SoF at injection point
rules.action: Freeze / review the chain · SoF + per-hop rationale RFI · SAR + exit on confirmed internal layering

rules.name: Collusive cluster / internal mule ring — on-us fan-in/fan-out (Mal2Mal)
rules.typology: Collusive account cluster / internal mule ring
rules.description: On-us many-to-one (fan-in) or one-to-many (fan-out) patterns across Mal accounts with shared device/IP and rapid pass-through — a collusive cluster or internal mule ring. Individual accounts may screen clear; the cluster is the signal (graph analytics).
rules.trigger: Fan-in / fan-out across many on-us accounts; shared device/IP; rapid pass-through; no economic rationale; scam-proceeds distribution shape
rules.thresholds: Graph analytics (edges, shared device/IP, new-edge re-clustering) · fan degree · pass-through velocity
rules.action: Freeze the cluster · graph review + SoF RFI to the hub · SAR + victim-aware exit of mule nodes

rules.name: Self-transfer limit-evasion structuring / self-layering (Mal2Mal)
rules.description: Same-person on-us moves between a customer's own Mal accounts used to stay under a limit (structuring) or to layer own funds (rapid in-then-out via a second own account). On-us convenience never lowers a floor; structuring across own/related accounts is itself a SAR typology.
rules.trigger: Repeated sub-threshold self-moves to evade a limit; rapid in-then-out via a second own account; aggregate own-account velocity off-profile
rules.thresholds: Same-person identity match · sub-threshold clustering · aggregate self-move velocity vs profile
rules.action: Step-up · aggregate-velocity monitoring · SAR on confirmed structuring / self-layering

rules.name: Owner personal/business commingling & round-tripping (Mal2Mal)
rules.description: Same-UBO on-us flow between an individual and their own Mal business used to commingle personal and business funds, obscure origin, or round-trip personal funds through the entity with no business rationale.
rules.trigger: Personal/business commingling to obscure origin; round-tripping personal funds through the owned business; owner injections with no business rationale
rules.thresholds: Ownership match (person = UBO) · SoF for injections · commingling flag · business-substance / rationale check
rules.action: SoF EDD · step-up · SAR on commingling / round-tripping to obscure origin

rules.name: Undeclared common-control intercompany round-trip — on-us (Mal2Mal)
rules.description: On-us 'intercompany' flow between two Mal entities secretly under one UBO, swept back and forth (circular A→B→A) to manufacture turnover with no external trade — undeclared common control. The group-relationship registry is mandatory before an intercompany purpose is selectable.
rules.trigger: Undeclared common control between on-us entities; circular A→B→A round-trips; gross ≫ net; turnover manufacturing with no external trade
rules.thresholds: Group-relationship registry (hard control, pre-selection) · common-control mapping · circular-flow detection (TM 5.9) · netting (gross vs net)
rules.action: Decline the flow until registry-declared · auditor-confirmed structure + netting RFI · SAR on undeclared control / round-trip

rules.name: On-us pair freeze — sanctioned / prohibited-type node (Mal2Mal)
rules.description: When either on-us party (or a linked node/UBO) becomes a confirmed SDN / 50%-owned nexus, or is a prohibited business type, BOTH legs of the on-us pair are frozen — on-us convenience never lowers a floor and a sanctioned/prohibited node freezes the pair.
rules.trigger: Confirmed SDN / 50%-owned / prohibited-jurisdiction nexus on either on-us node or UBO (continuous screening delta); prohibited business type as an on-us party
rules.thresholds: Continuous screening both parties (SDN delta ≤24h / other lists ≤48h) · 50%-rule · §6.4 prohibited-type screen
rules.action: Freeze BOTH legs of the on-us pair · no score · SAR + OFAC blocking report ≤10 business days (sanctions) · decline + exit (prohibited type)
